summary: High Renaissance Italian painter from Urbino. Most commonly identified by The School of Athens, La Disputa, and The Transfiguration. Student of Perugino. Painted numerous Madonnas (Goldfinch, Pinks, Sistine). The Pre-Raphaelite Brotherhood was named in opposition to his influence. Ingres painted him with La Fornarina. Died at 37.

works.name: The School of Athens (1509–1511)
works.description: Fresco in the Stanza della Segnatura, Vatican. Plato (modeled on Leonardo da Vinci) points upward while Aristotle gestures outward. Bramante modeled as Euclid using a compass at bottom right. Self-portrait of Raphael appears at right, depicted as Apelles. A six-pointed star is drawn on a small chalkboard by a figure analyzed by Guerino Mazzola. The tondo above reads 'causarum cognitio.' Anton Raphael Mengs made a copy titled Philosophy for the Duke of Northumberland. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Plato (modeled on Leonardo da Vinci) points upward while Aristotle gestures outward among a group of philosophers
examples: This artist painted Plato pointing upward while talking to Aristotle among a group of philosophers
name this Renaissance artist of The School of Athens
One of the two central figures holds a copy of his Nicomachean Ethics while the other, likely modeled after Leonardo da Vinci, points upwards

clues.clue: Bramante modeled as figure (Euclid) using a compass in the bottom right
examples: A man modeled on Bramante uses a compass in the bottom right
Vasari suggests that this artist was the model for Euclid in The School of Athens

examples: housed in the Stanza della Segnatura with The Parnassus and La Disputa

clues.clue: Raphael depicted himself as Apelles at the right edge; stands next to a man modeled on Protogenes

works.name: La Disputa (Disputation of the Holy Sacrament)
works.description: Fresco in the Stanza della Segnatura depicting Aquinas, Jerome, and theologians debating transubstantiation. Four putti hold the gospels below Christ's feet. Altar holds an ostensory. Hangs opposite The School of Athens.

clues.clue: Depicts Aquinas, Jerome, and theologians debating transubstantiation; four putti hold the gospels
examples: depicts Aquinas, Jerome, and many other theologians debating transubstantiation
four putti hold open copies of the gospels below Christ's feet

works.name: The Transfiguration (1516–1520)
works.description: Raphael's final painting. Upper half shows Moses and Elijah appearing; lower half shows apostles attempting to cure a possessed boy. A pope kept it instead of sending to Narbonne Cathedral.

clues.clue: Final painting; shows Moses and Elijah appearing above; apostles attempting to cure a possessed boy below
examples: this artist's final painting
this artist unusually included the apostles attempting to cure a possessed boy in the lower foreground

works.name: Sistine Madonna (Madonna di San Sisto, c. 1512)
works.description: Raphael painting moved from Piacenza to Dresden in 1754 by King Augustus III, who moved his throne to better view it. The Virgin and Child stand on clouds flanked by Pope Sixtus II (modeled on Julius II) and Saint Barbara. Two famous bored putti rest at the bottom parapet. Dozens of cherub faces emerge from the clouds in the background. A tiara with a Della Rovere acorn emblem rests on the parapet. Dostoyevsky kept a reproduction above his desk and would climb a chair to stare at a version for hours. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Two famous bored putti rest their elbows on the parapet at the bottom
examples: Two bored putti appear below the Virgin Mary in this Raphael painting
a pair of bored-looking putti resting on their elbows

clues.clue: Moved from Piacenza to Dresden in 1754 by King Augustus III (who moved his throne to view it better)
examples: In 1754, this painting was moved from Piacenza to Dresden after it was bought by King Augustus III for a massive twelve thousand zecchini
Belting described how Augustus III bringing this painting to Dresden caused it to dominate the German imagination

clues.clue: Saint Barbara kneels on the right; flanking figures include a golden-robed pope (modeled on Julius II) on the left
examples: A kneeling Saint Barbara looks downward, out of the frame, on the right of this painting
flanked by St. Barbara and a golden-robed pope

clues.clue: Dostoyevsky kept a reproduction above his desk; would climb a chair to stare at it for hours
examples: Fyodor Dostoyevsky would climb a chair to stare at it for hours, and a reproduction of it is hung above his desk at the Dostoyevsky museum in Moscow

clues.clue: Della Rovere tiara with acorn emblem rests on the wooden parapet at the bottom
examples: At the bottom of this painting, a tiara topped with an acorn, an emblem of the Della Rovere family, lies on a wooden parapet

clues.clue: Dozens of ghostly cherub faces emerge from the clouds in the background

Ghostly faces appear in the clouds behind the pope

works.name: The Fire in the Borgo (L'Incendio di Borgo, c. 1514–1517)
works.description: Fresco naming the Stanza dell'Incendio di Borgo (one of the Raphael Rooms). Depicts Leo IV extinguishing a fire from a loggia with his benediction. Left side shows a muscular nude dangling from a wall and another man carrying an old man on his back (inspired by Aeneas bearing Anchises). A woman in a yellow dress faces away from the viewer. Sydney Freedberg attributed the execution mainly to Raphael's student Giulio Romano. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Leo IV seen on balcony/loggia giving benediction to extinguish the fire; names the Stanza dell'Incendio di Borgo
examples: fresco depicting an episode from the Liber Pontificalis centering on Pope Leo IV, who is seen on a balcony in the background
names a room whose frescoes depict The Coronation of Charlemagne, The Battle of Ostia, and The Oath of Leo III

clues.clue: Old man carried on his back at left in scene inspired by Aeneas bearing Anchises; muscular nude dangles from wall nearby
examples: an old man wearing a Santa-hat-like cap is carried to safety in a scene inspired by Aeneas bearing Anchises away from Troy
a muscular nude dangles from a wall near another nude man carrying an old man on his back

clues.clue: Attributed mainly to Giulio Romano (student of Raphael) by Sydney Freedberg
examples: Sydney Freedberg attributed The Fire in the Borgo to this student of Raphael

works.name: Madonnas (Goldfinch, Pinks, Marriage of the Virgin)
works.description: Raphael painted numerous Madonna scenes. Madonna of the Goldfinch shows infants Jesus and John the Baptist playing with the title bird while Mary holds a book in a pastoral landscape. Marriage of the Virgin adapts Perugino's version with a Dome of the Rock-inspired building. Madonna of the Pinks (on fruitwood) shows a corner window looking onto a ruin. Other versions include Madonna of the Meadow, Cowper Madonnas, Madonna of the Ansidei, and Alba Madonna.

clues.clue: Madonna of the Goldfinch: Mary holds a book while infants Jesus and John the Baptist play with the title bird
examples: Mary holds a book in a pastoral landscape as she looks down at infants Jesus and John the Baptist, who are playing with the title bird

clues.clue: Marriage of the Virgin: adapts mentor Perugino's version; building inspired by Dome of the Rock
examples: this artist added more detail to a building inspired by the Dome of the Rock in the background of a wedding

clues.clue: Madonna of the Pinks (on fruitwood): central figures hold carnations; corner window looks onto a ruin
examples: a corner window looks out onto a ruin while the central figures each hold carnations

clues.clue: Other Madonnas: Madonna of the Meadow, Cowper Madonnas, Ansidei Madonna, Alba Madonna
examples: Raphael also painted versions of those paintings 'of the Meadow' and ones named for Sistine and Ansidei

comprehensive_summary:
Raphael (Raffaello Sanzio da Urbino, 1483–1520) was a High Renaissance Italian painter and architect, born in Urbino and trained under Pietro Perugino. He died at 37, having produced an enormous body of work in a short life.

His most famous work is The School of Athens (1509–1511), a fresco in the Vatican's Stanza della Segnatura. Plato, modeled on Leonardo da Vinci, points upward while Aristotle gestures outward among ancient philosophers. Bramante was modeled as Euclid using a compass in the bottom right. Raphael inserted a self-portrait as the painter Apelles at the right edge (standing beside a man modeled on Protogenes). Guerino Mazzola analyzed a six-pointed star drawn on a small chalkboard within the painting. The tondo above reads 'causarum cognitio.' Anton Raphael Mengs made a copy for the Duke of Northumberland titled Philosophy. The same room (Stanza della Segnatura) holds La Disputa (theologians including Aquinas and Jerome debating transubstantiation, with four putti holding the gospels below Christ's feet) and The Parnassus (Apollo playing a precursor to the violin, surrounded by Muses and poets, which inspired Mengs's own Parnassus fresco).

The Sistine Madonna (c. 1512) was moved from a Benedictine monastery in Piacenza to Dresden in 1754 by King Augustus III, who reportedly moved his throne to better view it. The Virgin and Child stand on clouds, flanked by Pope Sixtus II (modeled on Julius II) and Saint Barbara. Two famous bored putti rest on the parapet at the bottom; dozens of ghostly cherub faces emerge from the clouds. A Della Rovere tiara with acorn emblem lies on the parapet. Dostoyevsky kept a reproduction above his desk and would climb a chair to stare at a version for hours. Hans Belting analyzed this painting in Invisible Masterpiece.

The Fire in the Borgo (c. 1514–1517) is a fresco naming the Stanza dell'Incendio di Borgo; it shows Pope Leo IV giving a benediction from a loggia while crowds flee flames. At the left, a muscular nude dangles from a wall and a man carries an old man on his back in a pose echoing Aeneas bearing Anchises. Sydney Freedberg attributed much of the execution to Giulio Romano.

His final painting, The Transfiguration (1520), shows Moses and Elijah appearing above while apostles attempt to cure a possessed boy below. He painted numerous Madonnas: the Madonna of the Goldfinch (infants Jesus and John the Baptist playing with the bird while Mary holds a book), the Marriage of the Virgin (adapting Perugino's version with a Dome of the Rock-inspired building), and the Madonna of the Pinks (on fruitwood; central figures hold carnations; a corner window looks onto a ruin). The early Mond Crucifixion is Perugino-esque, with the sun and moon flanking I.N.R.I. and angels collecting Christ's blood in chalices. He also designed the Raphael Cartoons — tapestry designs depicting the lives of Peter and Paul.

The Pre-Raphaelite Brotherhood was named in opposition to his influence; Millais and Rossetti believed he 'ruined art.' Joshua Reynolds's praise of Raphael in his Discourses led to the nickname 'Sir Sloshua.' Ingres painted Raphael and his mistress La Fornarina five times over 35 years, with the third version including The Transfiguration in the background. Marcantonio Raimondi's engraving of Raphael's Judgement of Paris inspired Manet's Luncheon on the Grass (the trio in the bottom right). He inspired the German Nazarene movement; Benjamin West was called the 'American Raphael.' Baldassare Castiglione wrote a sonnet on Raphael's death and was closely associated with him at the court of Urbino. Federico Zuccaro complained in the margins of his copy of Vasari's Lives that Raphael was underpraise compared to Michelangelo.
